title: Fix clamp() lower bound
body:
## Problem

In `clamp_bug/mathutil.py`, `clamp(value, low, high)` does not enforce the lower bound.

When `value` is below `low`, it should return `low`. Currently `test_clamp_below_low` fails:

```
assert clamp(-5, 0, 10) == 0
```

## Expected

- if value < low → return low
- if value > high → return high
- otherwise → return value

Standard: `return max(low, min(high, value))` after normalizing swapped bounds.

## Acceptance

All tests in `tests/test_mathutil.py` pass.
notes: Local smoke fixture mirroring the Day-1 demo bug. Golden diff for edit-distance scoring.
